title: 2 Terminology
body_text:
2	Terminology
2.1	Definitions
Branch Number
A branch number is a 5-digit number used to identify the branch of a Canadian financial institution. The branch number and the institution number with a leading zero make up the transit number (e.g., 080912010 where 809 is the institution number and 12010 is the branch number).
Business Day
A business day is a weekday including Monday to Friday except for statutory holidays that fall on or are observed on a weekday.
Cash Parcel
A cash parcel is a package containing bank notes and/or coin shipped to or from financial institution locations (branch and/or ATM).
Client Centre
The Client Centre is a digital portal that provides secured access to Central 1芒聙聶s applications, and to information and news about our products and services.
Client Centre Users
Client Centre Users log in to the Client Centre to access certain applications. Client Centre Users can芒聙聶t manage CPS Admin Security Officers or other Client Centre Users.
Coin Box
A coin box is a box designed to hold coin rolls that is sturdy enough to not break in shipping.
Contaminated
Contaminated bank notes and/or coin are currency that pose a health hazard or safety risk because they have come into contact with toxic substances (e.g., blood, mould, drugs, and unknown substances) and can芒聙聶t be processed under normal operating procedures.
CPS Admin
The Central Portal System (CPS) Admin application is used to add, edit, and delete Client Centre Users and CPS Admin Security Officers.
CPS Admin Security Officer
CPS Admin Security Officers use the CPS Admin or the User Management application to add, edit, and delete Client Centre Users. They can also delete and modify other CPS Admin Security Officers (with some exceptions) via the User Management application. CPS Admin Security Officers use the Client Centre to log in to certain applications (e.g., Cash Services).
Currency
Currency usually includes bank notes and coin.
Currency Strap
A currency strap is a paper band that is wrapped around a bundle of 100 notes.
Delivery Date
The delivery date is the date the armoured car delivers the currency order to your financial institution location (branch and/or ATM).
Dispatch Date
The dispatch date is the date the currency is packaged at Royal Bank and picked up by armoured car.
Identification Stamp
An identification stamp is a stamp affixed to the back of a coin box by a financial institution that is involved in the exchange of that item. The identification stamp must include your financial institution芒聙聶s name, transit number, and address.
Joint Custody
Joint custody is the handling of an item or transaction under the direct observation of two people. Both persons are equally accountable for the item or transaction.
Late Order
A late order is an order that is submitted after the cut-off time (11 am local time) for regular currency orders on the Cash Services application.
Location
A location is the place to which orders are delivered. A branch can have multiple locations. For example, currency for a branch and currency for an ATM site may be delivered to the same address but are bundled separately. In this example, the branch has two locations.
Mutilated
Mutilated bank notes and/or coin are currency that show signs of excessive damage from fire or water, missing pieces, or other forms of deterioration. These notes and/or coin are likely too damaged or brittle to be used for payment or must be specially examined to determine their value.
Regular Order
A regular order is a one-time currency order that users enter in the Cash Services application.
Sell-back
A sell-back occurs when a customer sells back an item (i.e., precious metals) to the same financial institution it was originally purchased from.
ServiceNow
ServiceNow is the ticketing application used to report service issues, request changes to existing services, or submit questions to Central 1.
Standing Order
A standing order is a recurring currency order that is automatically submitted based on your financial institution芒聙聶s request. This type of order is beneficial if a financial institution芒聙聶s currency needs are consistent from week to week.
Transit Number
A transit number is the unique code used to identify financial institutions in Canada. A transit number consists of the 3-digit institution number and the 5-digit branch number with a leading zero (e.g., 080912010).
Unfit Bank Notes
Unfit bank notes are notes that are not suitable for circulation because of their physical condition (e.g., note is torn and/or has holes).
User Management
User Management is an application used to add, edit, and delete Client Centre Users. It is also used to edit and delete other CPS Admin Security Officers and to manage 2-step security tokens. Eventually, User Management will replace CPS Admin.
2.2	Frequently Asked Questions
Set-up and Access
Question 1
Which browsers are compatible with the Cash Services application?
Answer
The official supported browser is Microsoft Edge. However, Cash Services functions normally in other browsers as well (e.g., Safari, Chrome, Firefox).
Question 2
Can I use my Client Centre user ID to access Cash Services?
Answer
No. To access Cash Services, you will use a numerical ID that is generated when an Admin or Access Management User creates your account in Cash Services.
Question 3
Do users have limits?
Answer
No. In Cash Services, the limits are set per location. The location limit includes the combined total of Canadian notes and coin and US notes (at face value). If a location limit is set to $100K, users with access to that location can order any combination of US and Canadian currency on a given day, provided the sum does not exceed $100K.
Question 4
What are the password constraints for Cash Services?
Answer
At initial login, users are prompted to change their password. The password must be 8 to 20 characters and it must contain
at least one capital letter,
one number, and
one of the following characters: @, #, $, %.
Users are prompted to change their passwords every 60 days.
Question 5
If the account is locked, does it automatically unlock after a certain time?
Answer
No. After three unsuccessful login attempts an account is locked. Only Admin or Access Management Users can unlock accounts.
Question 6
Are there restrictions on how many locations an employee can be authorized to order cash at?
Answer
No. Users can be granted permission to work at one, some, or all your locations.
Question 7
When ordering cash, do we have to order Canadian and US currency separately?
Answer
No. An order can include both currencies.
Question 8
What is the cut-off time for ordering cash?
Answer
The order placement cut-off time is 11:00 am local time for dispatch on the next business day. Orders placed after 11:00 am will have an extra day added before the order can be dispatched.
Question 9
Is the delivery date the date that a shipment leaves Royal Bank or is it the date our financial institution receives the order?
Answer
The dispatch date is the date the armoured car picks up the cash from Royal Bank.
The delivery date is the date the cash order arrives at a location.
Question 10
Will the delivery dates default to what that location has for set delivery?
Answer
Yes. They will also take into consideration the days in transit for that location.
Question 11
Is there a validation in place for the cash orders?
Answer
Yes. All orders must balance before they are submitted. This means that the amount of the units entered in the order section must add up to the total value of the order.
If the amounts don芒聙聶t match, or if the value of the order is higher than the daily limit set for that location, the order can芒聙聶t be submitted.
Question 12
Can we amend orders?
Answer
Yes. Orders in the Pending & Ready category in Cash Services can be modified or deleted.
After the cut-off time at 11:00 am local time, one business day before the dispatch date, orders are moved to the Carrier Check & Released category, where they can be viewed, but no longer edited or deleted.
Question 13
Is there a function in Cash Services for Standing Orders, Late Orders, or Special Orders?
Answer
Not yet. These orders are currently processed in ServiceNow.
Question 14
Does the system provide an error message on late orders?
Answer
You can芒聙聶t make late orders in Cash Services. The application will always provide the earliest delivery date possible. If you need an order before that date, submit a ServiceNow ticket.
Question 15
Will our account be debited the same day that a cash order is submitted in Cash Services? What time will the debit appear?
Answer
Central 1 debits your financial institution the day that the order is sent out from Royal Bank (dispatch date).
So, if the order is entered in Cash Services on July 20 and sent out by Royal Bank on July 21, the debit would appear on July 21, between 5 pm and 11 pm PT (8 pm and 2 am ET).
Question 16
If a branch returns cash today, when will our account be credited?
Answer
Central 1 credits your financial institution account between 5 pm and 11 pm PT (8 pm and 2 am ET) on the day the cash is received and processed at the Royal Bank cash centre.
Question 17
Is there a new description for the Cash Returns/Orders (with the new ID number instead of the transit)?
Answer
The new ID number will be implemented in the second phase of the project. In the meantime, the Cash Returns/Orders description on the reports and in the current account in Current Account Services (CAS) will remain the same.
Question 18
What do the reports/monthly statements capture?
Answer
We are providing the following two updated reports:
Monthly Cash Ordering & Returns statement (CSV) 芒聙聯 Displays all transactions (orders & returns) for the previous month; available on the File and Report Exchange in your financial institution芒聙聶s root directory (main folder).
Daily Cash Ordering & Returns statement (CSV) 芒聙聯 Displays all transactions (orders & returns) processed for the day; available on the File and Report Exchange in your financial institution芒聙聶s root directory (main folder).
Question 19
Are the shipping charges part of the monthly fees?
Answer
No. Armoured car fees are not included in the fees.
Question 20
How are fees shown on the recap?
Answer
Fees are calculated at the end of the month, then debited from your financial institution芒聙聶s current account.
END
